name: Progrowth Bank
description_short: ProGrowth Bank is a locally-owned FDIC-insured bank operating in Southern Minnesota with three locations, offering personal and business banking, CDs, money market savings, and loans.
description_long:
ProGrowth Bank is a community-focused financial institution based in Southern Minnesota with a long tradition of serving customers through different life phases. The bank is locally owned by Phil and Sue Keithahn, who emphasize local decision-making and community involvement rather than corporate policy. The institution operates three branch locations in Gaylord, Nicollet, and Mankato, serving the Greater Nicollet Area and Mankato communities with hands-on service from experienced staff members.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking products. On the deposit side, they provide Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 9 to 61 months (rates from 3.45% to 3.90% APY as of March 2026), Platinum Money Market Savings accounts earning up to 3.51% APY on balances of $250,000+, and standard checking and savings accounts. Loan products include personal loans for vehicles, mortgages, and general purposes, as well as dedicated business and agricultural lending services. The bank also serves non-profit organizations with specialized products. Digital conveniences include online banking, mobile banking access, and a network of ATMs.

ProGrowth Bank distinguishes itself through genuine local ownership and decision-making, contrasting with regional or national banks. The owners are active community members—Sue Keithahn has received the Sertoma Service to Mankind Award, YWCA Women of Distinction Award, and is a KNUJ Hometown Hero; Phil Keithahn has served as a youth wrestling coach and Boy Scout leader for over 20 years. The bank explicitly markets itself as "Locally Owned. Local Decision Making" and emphasizes that staff members provide quality service. They offer relationship rewards programs and educational content through their Money IQ videos. FDIC insurance backing provides standard federal deposit protection.

As a small regional bank with only three locations, ProGrowth Bank's reach and product sophistication are limited compared to larger institutions. While CD and money market rates are competitive for early 2026, they may not lead in all terms. The $10,000 minimum balance requirement for advertised CD rates and $10,000 average ledger balance for money market accounts exclude smaller depositors from top rates. Geographic limitation to Southern Minnesota means this bank is suitable primarily for residents in or near Gaylord, Nicollet, or Mankato. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with terms from 9 to 61 months
Platinum Money Market Savings accounts with tiered rates based on balance
Personal loans (vehicle loans, mortgages, general purpose loans)
Financial education through Money IQ videos
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Genuinely locally owned with owners (Phil and Sue Keithahn) actively involved in community organizations and volunteer work
Competitive CD rates ranging from 3.45% to 3.90% APY across multiple terms (as of March 2026)
Money Market Savings earning 3.51% APY on balances $250,000+ with multiple tiered rates
Comprehensive loan products including personal, auto, mortgage, business, and agricultural lending
Relationship Rewards program that provides benefits for banking customers
Multiple service channels including online banking, mobile banking, and physical ATM network
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three locations in Southern Minnesota (Gaylord, Nicollet, Mankato), making accessibility difficult for non-local customers
$10,000 minimum balance requirement to earn advertised CD and money market rates excludes smaller savers from top yields
Early withdrawal penalties apply to CDs, and the website indicates fees could reduce overall earnings
No mention of premium checking accounts, investment services, credit cards, or wealth management products offered by larger banks
Limited branch network means fewer in-person service options compared to regional or national banks
best_for: Residents of Gaylord, Nicollet, or Mankato, Minnesota seeking community-focused banking with local decision-makers
Savers with $10,000+ looking for competitive CD rates and money market accounts in a stable, FDIC-insured institution
Small business owners and agricultural enterprises in Southern Minnesota needing personalized lending and local relationships
Community-minded customers who prioritize supporting locally-owned businesses and owner involvement in civic organizations
diagnosis: ProGrowth Bank is best suited for Southern Minnesota residents with substantial savings ($10,000+) who value genuine local ownership, community involvement, and personalized service over breadth of digital products or national convenience. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank operates only three physical locations and is not accessible to customers outside the Gaylord, Nicollet, and Mankato areas, and limited branch network reduces service convenience compared to larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
